title: Interaction-aware neighbor-gap features from game-theoretic pit timing
summary: Recent F1 strategy literature emphasizes that pit decisions depend on opponents, traffic, and track-position interactions, and neglecting competition can materially reduce strategy quality.
rationale: Recent F1 strategy literature emphasizes that pit decisions depend on opponents, traffic, and track-position interactions, and neglecting competition can materially reduce strategy quality. Your current best features are mostly self-state features, so explicit local-rival context is still underused.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year` and lap, create relative features versus nearby cars: percentile/rank of `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, position-local gaps (cars ahead/behind by 1-2 places), neighbor compound/stint mismatches, and 'pit now vs rivals still out' proxies such as own tyre-age minus median of top-5 nearby positions. Train as extra features in the existing tree model first.
expected_effect: Could improve discrimination on borderline strategic laps where raw wear is ambiguous but local traffic/undercut pressure makes a stop more or less likely.
risk: Position is not a true time-gap signal, so some traffic proxies may be noisy; gains depend on careful past-only grouping within race-lap slices.
